message:
refactor(orphans): replace ad-hoc if-else output chain with OrphanRenderMode enum

The orphans command previously used a cascade of `if ctx.is_json()` /
`if ctx.is_toon()` / `if ctx.is_rich()` checks to decide how to render
output, and the `json` parameter was silently ignored (`_json: bool`).
This made the output dispatch hard to follow and caused `--robot` mode
to behave inconsistently when invoked through `execute_with_storage_ctx`
(the preopened-storage path used by the main CLI entry point).

Changes:

- Introduce `OrphanRenderMode` enum (Quiet/Json/Toon/Rich/Plain) and a
  `const fn resolve_render_mode(json, output_mode)` that collapses the
  `--json`/`--robot` flag with the inherited `OutputMode` into a single
  discriminant. The `json` flag takes priority: when true, it overrides
  any inherited mode to `Json`.

- Thread the `json: bool` parameter (previously unused `_json`) through
  `execute` -> `execute_inner` and add it to the
  `execute_with_storage_ctx` public API. The call site in main.rs now
  passes `cli.json || args.robot`, matching the convention already used
  by the defer/undefer commands.

- Replace both the main rendering block and `output_empty` with
  exhaustive `match render_mode { ... }` arms. This eliminates the
  `emit_json_output` helper (which created a throwaway OutputContext)
  in favour of direct `serde_json::to_string_pretty` for robot-mode
  JSON when the shared OutputContext isn't itself in JSON mode.

- Remove the now-unnecessary `args.robot` reads from output_empty; the
  render mode already encodes that information.

- Add 7 unit tests covering resolve_render_mode: robot flag overrides
  every inherited mode to Json, and each OutputMode is preserved when
  the robot flag is false.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
